meaning: n.低地的人,苏格兰低地地方的人
examples: I'm growing mine as a bedroom lowlander and it seems to be adjusting well.
But if a lowlander stays in this high-altitude environment for too long, the adaptation starts to turn against his or her health.
A lowlander exposed to the low oxygen concentrations of high elevations can quickly fall victim to altitude sickness, whose symptoms include nausea, dizziness, and fatigue.
Although Sir Ludovic's father was a Lowlander and his mother a Highlander, an English childhood and an English accent made him consider himself an Anglo-Scot.
